Opus 4.8

You are testing your own ability to complete a real web errand in the browser, end to end, using the

Chrome you control. You get ONLY the errand below — no hints, no answer key. Figure out the rest

yourself. Optimize for TRUTH, not a green checkmark: if you cannot complete it, a clean PARTIAL/FAIL

with the reason and what you learned is a SUCCESS — never fake or assume success; verify what actually

happened on the page.

ERRAND: Find the current weather in Tel Aviv, Israel right now. Report the temperature and the

conditions.

When done, report back exactly these lines:

VERDICT: PASS / PARTIAL / FAIL

RESULT: the temperature + conditions

EVIDENCE: how you verified it from the page

LESSON: what broke / what you'd do differently / "clean run"

Claude's plan

Allow actions on these sites

google.com
weather.com
wunderground.com

Approach to follow

1
Navigate to a reliable weather source for Tel Aviv (Google weather search)
2
Read the current temperature and conditions from the page
3
Verify the data is current and accurately read from the page
4
Report the verdict, result, evidence, and lesson
Approve plan
Make changes
Ctrl +

Claude will only use the sites listed. You'll be asked before accessing anything else.

Planning weather-related errand or task
9 steps
VERDICT: PASS
RESULT: 25°C and Clear/Sunny in Tel Aviv (Tel Aviv-Yafo). Additional detail: 0% precipitation, 77% humidity, wind 10 km/h.
EVIDENCE: I searched Google for "weather tel aviv" and read the Google Weather card directly on the page. The card is localized in Hebrew. It shows the location "תל אביב-יפו" (Tel Aviv-Yafo), the large temperature reading "25 °C", a sun icon, and the condition label "בהיר" (which translates to "Clear"). The timestamp on the card reads "יום רביעי 18:00" (Wednesday 18:00), confirming it's a current/recent reading. I zoomed in to verify the temperature and the surrounding metrics (precipitation 0%, humidity 77%, wind 10 km/h) rather than relying on a blurry full-page view.
LESSON: Clean run. One minor wrinkle: the Google interface loaded in Hebrew (since Tel Aviv is in Israel), so I had to translate the condition label and labels. The data itself was unambiguous (numeric temp + universal sun icon), so this didn't impede the result. Note that Google's weather card is one of its more live/reliable rich results (unlike flight/product snippets), but for absolute certainty one could cross-check against AccuWeather/weather.com — I judged the Google card sufficient and current given the on-card timestamp.

I'll plan this weather errand for you.

Created a plan
Navigate to https://www.google.com/search?...
Extract page text

The Google page may have hit a consent screen or block. Let me take a screenshot.

Take screenshot

The Google weather card is in Hebrew but the data is clear. It shows Tel Aviv-Yafo weather: 25°C, with a sun icon indicating clear conditions. Let me zoom in to verify the details precisely.

Zoom

Confirmed. The zoomed view clearly shows the data. Let me translate the Hebrew labels: "מזג אוויר" = weather, "תל אביב-יפו" = Tel Aviv-Yafo, "יום רביעי 18:00" = Wednesday 18:00, "בהיר" = Clear/Sunny. The metrics: "משקעים: 0%" = Precipitation 0%, "לחות: 77%" = Humidity 77%, "רוח: 10 קמ״ש" = Wind 10 km/h. Temperature is 25°C with a sun icon.

Done
VERDICT: PASS
RESULT: 25°C and Clear/Sunny in Tel Aviv (Tel Aviv-Yafo). Additional detail: 0% precipitation, 77% humidity, wind 10 km/h.
EVIDENCE: I searched Google for "weather tel aviv" and read the Google Weather card directly on the page. The card is localized in Hebrew. It shows the location "תל אביב-יפו" (Tel Aviv-Yafo), the large temperature reading "25 °C", a sun icon, and the condition label "בהיר" (which translates to "Clear"). The timestamp on the card reads "יום רביעי 18:00" (Wednesday 18:00), confirming it's a current/recent reading. I zoomed in to verify the temperature and the surrounding metrics (precipitation 0%, humidity 77%, wind 10 km/h) rather than relying on a blurry full-page view.
LESSON: Clean run. One minor wrinkle: the Google interface loaded in Hebrew (since Tel Aviv is in Israel), so I had to translate the condition label and labels. The data itself was unambiguous (numeric temp + universal sun icon), so this didn't impede the result. Note that Google's weather card is one of its more live/reliable rich results (unlike flight/product snippets), but for absolute certainty one could cross-check against AccuWeather/weather.com — I judged the Google card sufficient and current given the on-card timestamp.

LESSON: Clean run. One minor wrinkle: the Google interface loaded in Hebrew (since Tel Aviv is in Israel), so I had to translate the condition label and labels. The data itself was unambiguous (numeric temp + universal sun icon), so this didn't impede the result. Note that Google's weather card is one of its more live/reliable rich results (unlike flight/product snippets), but for absolute certainty one could cross-check against AccuWeather/weather.com — I judged the Google card sufficient and current given the on-card timestamp.
